inline_comments.message: These files are deleted and then added. Using git command "git mv", it marks files as moved, thus making the review much smaller and makes the reviewing process much simpler.
I see no such service in aai all-services....
Not sure gremlin is part of aai, maybe it is not needed.
Actually, the service is still sparky-be. Sorry for the confusion.
Apparently the service is still hbase. Sorry for the confusion.

Okay, I'll add aai- there. 
ubuntu@kll5032:~/oom/kubernetes/aai/templates$ kubectl get services --all-namespaces | grep elastic
onap          aai-elasticsearch            ClusterIP   None            <none>        9200/TCP                                                                     4d
onap          elasticsearch                NodePort    10.43.157.45    <none>        9200:30254/TCP                                                               4d
onap          elasticsearchtcp             ClusterIP   10.43.138.18    <none>        9300/TCP                                                                     4d

This one is for pod: 
ubuntu@kll5032:~/oom/kubernetes/aai/templates$ kubectl get services --all-namespaces | grep data-router
ubuntu@kll5032:~/oom/kubernetes/aai/templates$
ubuntu@kll5032:~$  kubectl get pods --all-namespaces | grep data-router
onap          aai-data-router-6fbb8695d4-v78l7               1/1       Running            0          4d

Looks gremlin is not part of aai any more, there is no such app, I will remove this check script. 
While there is a still service gremlin, should we remove this gremlin service definition? 
ubuntu@kll5032:~/oom/kubernetes/aai/templates$ grep -i gremlin *.yaml
all-services.yaml:  name: gremlin
all-services.yaml:    app: gremlin
all-services.yaml:  - name: "gremlin-port"
all-services.yaml:    app: gremlin
ubuntu@kll5032:~/oom/kubernetes/aai/templates$ kubectl get services --all-namespaces | grep gremlin
onap          gremlin                      ClusterIP   None            <none>        8182/TCP                                                                     4d

Yes the service is sparky-be, while the pod name is aai-sparky-be: 
ubuntu@kll5032:~$ kubectl get pods --all-namespaces | grep sparky
onap          aai-sparky-be-57bd9944b5-hxdtf                 2/2       Running            0          4d
ubuntu@kll5032:~$ kubectl get services --all-namespaces | grep sparky
onap          sparky-be                    ClusterIP   None            <none>        9517/TCP                                                                     4d

Okay, I'll change it to hbase. 
the pod name is still aai-hbase though, should pod name be same as service name? 

ubuntu@kll5032:~$  kubectl get services --all-namespaces | grep hbase
onap          hbase                        ClusterIP   None            <none>        2181/TCP,8080/TCP,8085/TCP,9090/TCP,16000/TCP,16010/TCP,16201/TCP            4d
ubuntu@kll5032:~$  kubectl get pods --all-namespaces | grep hbase
onap          aai-hbase-5bcb5884d4-v72tb                     1/1       Running            0          4d
